title: OPAL Multiplex Immunofluorescence Protocol for 4-Plex Staining
author: Franz Zemp
materials: Rehydration. Rehydrate sections through a graded ethanol series:
1X AR6 buffer for GPNMB and CD31
1X AR9 buffer for CD155 and CD112

steps.title: Section Preparation
steps.instruction: Section Preparation. Cut FFPE tissue samples into 5 µm thick sections and mount onto charged slides.

steps.title: Deparaffinization
steps.instruction: Deparaffinization. Incubate slides for 3 hours at 60°C. Perform three washes in xylene, 5 minutes each.

steps.title: Heat-Induced Antigen Retrieval (HIER)
steps.instruction: Heat-Induced Antigen Retrieval (HIER). Perform antigen retrieval in a pressure cooker for 22 minutes using the appropriate buffer:

steps.title: Allow slides to cool to room temperature in the buffer
steps.instruction: Allow slides to cool to room temperature in the buffer.

steps.title: Rinse in distilled water followed by 1× TBST
steps.instruction: Rinse in distilled water followed by 1× TBST.

steps.title: Non-Specific Binding Block
steps.instruction: Non-Specific Binding Block. Apply 200 µL of Protein Block (Agilent, X090930-2) to each section. Incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature. Gently remove excess blocking solution (do not rinse).

steps.title: Multiplex Immunofluorescence Staining
steps.instruction: Multiplex Immunofluorescence Staining. Perform sequential multiplex staining according to the manufacturer’s instructions for the Opal 6-Plex Manual Detection Kit (Akoya Biosciences). Apply primary antibodies and corresponding Opal fluorophores in the following order and repeat step 4-6 until all markers are stained.

steps.title: CD155 (1:100) – incubate overnight at...
steps.instruction: CD155 (1:100) – incubate overnight at 4°C, paired with Opal 520 (1:200)

steps.title: GPNMB (1:200) – incubate 1 hour...
steps.instruction: GPNMB (1:200) – incubate 1 hour at room temperature, paired with Opal 570 (1:200)

steps.title: CD31 (1:100) – incubate overnight at...
steps.instruction: CD31 (1:100) – incubate overnight at 4°C, paired with Opal 690 (1:200)

steps.title: CD112 (1:200) – incubate overnight at...
steps.instruction: CD112 (1:200) – incubate overnight at 4°C, paired with Opal 780 (1:25) Include appropriate stripping and washing steps between each antibody cycle as recommended by the Opal kit protocol.

steps.title: Slide Scanning
steps.instruction: Slide Scanning. Scan stained slides using the Akoya Vectra Polaris at 20× resolution.

steps.title: Image Analysis
steps.instruction: Image Analysis. Import scanned images into HALO software (Indica Labs, v4.0.5107.407) for visualization, spectral unmixing, and quantitative analysis of marker expression, co-localization, and cellular phenotyping.
